%% S4: EXACT-TV vs RQL median-gap grid + pair statistics

\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Complete EXACT-TV versus RQL comparison on the frozen $8^2$/$16^2$ grid (864 paired reconstructions). Cells give the median radiometric-PSNR gap $\mathrm{PSNR}(\text{EXACT-TV})-\mathrm{PSNR}(\text{RQL})$ in dB per $(\text{side},\bar\rho,\nu)$ (pair count in parentheses; three seeds $\times$ six scenes per cell). The median gap stays within $0.011$~dB in magnitude throughout the deployment zone ($\bar\rho\le1$) and reaches only $-0.05$~dB in the extreme-load corner ($16^2$, $\bar\rho\ge0.6$, $\nu=2000$). Over all pairs: 141 exactly identical, median $+0.00$~dB, maximum $|{\rm gap}|=9.23$~dB, and only $9.0\%$ of pairs exceed $0.1$~dB in either direction -- consistent with the nonconvexity of the exact objective and with no systematic advantage to either arm.}
\label{tab:s4_grid}
